name: Tamashiro Market
tagline: Honolulu's most trusted fish market for over 80 years — where local families buy poke and fresh seafood for parties.
parking: Small lot in front; street parking on N King St
talkStory: The Tamashiro family opened this Kalihi fish market in the 1940s, originally serving the neighborhood's Japanese and Filipino fishing families. The poke counter -- with a dozen daily varieties of ahi, tako, and seasonal specials -- is arguably the best on Oahu. The hand-painted exterior murals have become a Kalihi landmark. Come Saturday before 10am for the freshest selection; by noon, the popular cuts are gone and locals are eating styrofoam plates standing in the parking lot.
